message:
fix(sync): recover from mutex poisoning instead of cascading panics

Replace every .lock().expect("...poisoned...") with
.lock().unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner) across the runtime,
scheduler, sharded state, sync primitives, and benchmarks.

Mutex poisoning occurs when a thread panics while holding a lock. The
previous behavior caused every subsequent lock attempt to also panic,
creating a cascade where one task's panic brought down the entire
runtime. The new pattern recovers the inner data from the PoisonError,
allowing other threads to continue operating on the (potentially
partially-modified) state.

This is especially important for an async runtime where task panics
should be isolated — a panicked task should not prevent other tasks
from making progress through shared scheduler and state structures.

Affected modules: runtime/builder, runtime/io_driver,
runtime/scheduler/{local_queue,three_lane,worker},
runtime/sharded_state, sync/{contended_mutex,once_cell},
benches/cancel_drain_bench.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
